title: PRD and GSD artifacts remain source of truth
summary: Architecture registry JSONL remains a derived projection over PRD, GSD, ADR, source, and runtime evidence.
verification: Verifier rejects records or docs that claim JSONL/GraphML are authoritative over source docs.
non_claims: Does not make generated artifacts authoritative.

title: Deterministic architecture extractor check
summary: The S02 extractor check reports stale generated JSONL, missing anchors, malformed S08 JSON, and unsafe status/proof mappings.
verification: `uv run python scripts/extract-prd-architecture-items.py --check` exits zero on current generated outputs.
non_claims: Extractor check is not product runtime proof.

title: Legal Nexus orchestrator component boundary
summary: Legal Nexus is the planned Python orchestration boundary for deterministic lookup, Legal KnowQL parsing, policy checks, and FalkorDB access.
verification: Future implementation defines public API methods, access-control boundaries, and runtime diagnostics before product claims.
non_claims: Does not implement Legal Nexus runtime behavior.
Does not prove product Legal KnowQL behavior.

title: Core legal evidence entities
summary: LegalAct, ActEdition, SourceDocument, SourceBlock, EvidenceSpan, and NormStatement are core source-backed concepts to be represented before product extraction or retrieval claims are upgraded.
verification: Future implementation records concrete graph schema fields and validates source/evidence linkage on real parser outputs.
non_claims: Does not assert final legal graph schema completeness.

title: Temporal property bundle
summary: Edition date, validity period, effective period, status, and temporal confidence are central temporal semantics for LegalGraph records.
verification: Future temporal proof validates field semantics on real document editions and query-time as-of behavior.
non_claims: Does not specify temporal storage implementation.
Does not validate temporal conflict resolution.

title: Use docs-as-code architecture registry
summary: Use JSONL architecture item and edge records generated from PRD/GSD/ADR sources, analyzed through NetworkX, and verified by executable checks.
verification: Architecture registry extractor/checks, graph builder, and verifier pass.
non_claims: JSONL and GraphML are not source-of-truth replacements.

considered_options.title: Docs-as-code registry
considered_options.summary: Use tracked JSONL plus verifier as a derived projection.

considered_options.title: GSD-only architecture state
considered_options.summary: Keep architecture state only in GSD artifacts and memory.
cons: Harder to review through git because .gsd is symlinked

positive_consequences: Architecture claims become traceable and checkable.
negative_consequences: Schema drift must be controlled by tests.
assumptions: Tracked prd/architecture artifacts can be reviewed safely in git.
constraints: Do not treat derived graph artifacts as authoritative.
implications: Future architecture changes should update the registry or source anchors.

title: Add architecture verification router skill in S05
summary: Create a compact project-local legalgraph-architecture-verification router skill after the schema and verifier stabilize.
verification: S05 skill consistency test passes and references current verifier paths.
non_claims: The skill is guidance, not a source of truth.

considered_options.title: Create skill in S05
considered_options.summary: Wait until schema and verifier stabilize.

considered_options.title: Create skill immediately
considered_options.summary: Write guidance before schema and verifier exist.

positive_consequences: Future architecture work has a compact router.
negative_consequences: S05 must maintain skill consistency checks.

title: Consultant relation-candidate evidence
summary: Tracked Consultant WordML relation-candidate artifacts preserve candidate relation identity without asserting legal relation correctness.
verification: `uv run python scripts/build-consultant-relation-candidates.py --check` verifies Consultant candidate artifact freshness.
non_claims: Does not prove Consultant relation correctness.

title: Consultant full-act hierarchy parser proof
summary: M009 establishes Consultant Plus WordML as the primary full-normative-act parser source for a single 44-FZ tracer and emits 2185 bounded hierarchy records with provenance and proof-package guardrails.
verification: `build-consultant-hierarchy-records.py --check`, prior-art comparison `--check`, and `test_consultant_parser_proof.py` pass for the Consultant-primary/Garant-deferred bounded proof package.
non_claims: Does not prove multi-document Consultant expansion.
Does not prove Garant ODT parser regression.
Does not prove product ETL readiness.
Does not prove FalkorDB loading/runtime behavior.

title: Bounded parser/retrieval golden-test proof
summary: M008 establishes an executable five-case golden-test harness over tracked M006 parser artifacts with fail-closed evaluator behavior and explicit non-authoritative boundaries.
verification: `.venv/bin/python3 -m pytest tests/test_parser_golden_contract.py tests/test_parser_golden_cases.py tests/test_parser_golden_evaluator.py tests/test_parser_golden_proof_report.py` and evaluator `--check` evidence pass for bounded scope.
non_claims: Does not prove product retrieval quality.
Does not prove citation-safe retrieval readiness.
Does not prove FalkorDB loading/runtime behavior.

title: Bounded ODT smoke-record evidence
summary: Tracked ODT smoke records preserve raw content.xml order and capped SourceBlockRecord rows for canonical Garant ODT fixtures.
verification: `uv run python scripts/build-odt-smoke-records.py --check` verifies ODT smoke artifact freshness.
non_claims: No final legal hierarchy extraction claim.

title: Parser record contract evidence
summary: Typed parser records define deterministic non-authoritative DocumentRecord, SourceBlockRecord, and RelationCandidateRecord boundaries.
verification: `uv run python scripts/validate-parser-records.py --check` verifies schemas, examples, and contract freshness.
non_claims: Does not prove product ETL readiness.

title: Parser source fixture inventory evidence
summary: M006 fixture inventory establishes canonical parser source paths and hygiene diagnostics for downstream parser work.
verification: `uv run python scripts/inventory-parser-fixtures.py --check` verifies fixture inventory freshness and hygiene.

title: Parser NetworkX staging graph evidence
summary: The NetworkX staging graph preserves validated parser JSONL records and keyed relation-candidate edges without loading FalkorDB.
verification: `uv run python scripts/build-parser-staging-graph.py --check` verifies staging graph freshness and invariants.
non_claims: Does not prove FalkorDB loading/runtime behavior.

title: GraphRAG/FalkorDB mathematical analysis research input
summary: Imported research argues for deterministic graph pre-filtering, temporal legal graph reasoning, and graph algorithms before LLM synthesis, but remains a non-authoritative planning input.
verification: Assessment classifies ideas into applicable-now principles, proof-gated candidates, and deferred/not-adopted claims; future proof must validate any runtime, SDK, benchmark, or retrieval-quality claim.
non_claims: Does not prove product retrieval quality.
Does not prove FalkorDB production-scale behavior.
Does not validate benchmark, cost, or latency claims.

title: Habr Legal RAG iteration and scaling research input
summary: Imported Habr case study highlights format-first validation, citation precision, graph-constrained retrieval, no-answer evaluation, and scale/noise testing as proof-required candidates for LegalGraph Nexus.
verification: Human-reviewed JSON comparison classifies all transferable ideas as requiring project-specific verification before adoption; future proof must validate retrieval IDs, evidence precision, no-answer behavior, scale/noise degradation, and any runtime or model claim.
non_claims: Does not prove product retrieval quality.
Does not prove FalkorDB runtime/vector/full-text/rerank behavior.
Does not authorize generated Cypher execution.

title: Embedding model supply-chain integrity gate
summary: Local/open-weight embedding candidates require provenance, artifact integrity, resource, and leakage checks before promotion.
verification: Future embedding proof records model source, checksum or revision, local runtime envelope, vector dimension, and no-secret/no-raw-vector leakage checks.
non_claims: Does not promote any embedding model to product default.
Does not allow managed embedding API fallback.
Does not prove product retrieval quality.

title: Temporal same-date multi-edition conflict policy
summary: Temporal same-date and multi-edition legal conflict policy remains unresolved and must stay visible.
verification: A future proof slice defines and verifies same-date/multi-edition conflict policy.
non_claims: Does not validate temporal conflict resolution.

title: Product parser and retrieval readiness gate
summary: M008 validates a bounded executable golden-test harness, but product parser completeness, citation-safe retrieval, and retrieval quality remain unresolved.
verification: Future product proof demonstrates parser completeness boundaries, citation-safe retrieval behavior, and retrieval quality over real legal source fixtures.

title: Local embedding quality proof
summary: Local embedding baseline is bounded and still needs product retrieval quality proof.
verification: Retrieval quality benchmark passes under local/open-weight embedding constraints.
non_claims: No managed embedding API fallback claim.

title: FalkorDBLite to Docker migration runbook
summary: FalkorDBLite to Docker migration remains a deployment/operability proof gate.
verification: Migration runbook is executed against bounded fixtures and runtime diagnostics.

title: Generated-Cypher safety and validation gate
summary: Generated-Cypher planning records a safety gate for read-only, schema-grounded, evidence-returning, deterministic validation before any future execution path.
verification: A future product proof demonstrates validator acceptance/rejection behavior across representative Legal KnowQL tasks and live graph schemas.
non_claims: Does not prove provider generation quality.
Does not prove production Legal KnowQL behavior.
Does not authorize executing raw generated Cypher.

title: Legal Nexus access-control proof gate
summary: Legal Nexus API access control, authentication, and authorization remain unproven product surfaces until explicit runtime checks exist.
verification: Future security proof defines caller boundaries, authorization policy, audit logging, and denial diagnostics for Legal Nexus operations.
non_claims: Does not assert current product is insecure.
Does not define a production API surface.

title: M001 architecture-only guardrail
summary: M001 remains architecture-only and must not ship product runtime behavior.
verification: S08 verifier rejects overclaim markers that promote architecture findings into product runtime or legal-quality claims.

title: Deterministic observability and auditability baseline
summary: Architecture work must preserve deterministic diagnostics, auditability, and explicit non-claim surfaces before runtime services are promoted.
verification: Future implementation defines measurable audit/latency/failure-state checks and verifies them against runtime surfaces.

title: Architecture review finding classification
summary: Architecture review reports must classify PRD claims with severity, evidence, impact, recommendation, owner, and roadmap effect.
verification: Review S08 final report and S08-FINDINGS.json before using architecture conclusions.
non_claims: No product Legal KnowQL behavior claim.
No managed embedding API fallback claim.

title: Architecture findings require owner and verification criteria
summary: Discovered architecture issues must carry owner, resolution path, and verification criteria.
verification: Check generated items and S08 findings for owner, resolution, and verification text.
non_claims: No product Legal KnowQL behavior claim.
No managed embedding API fallback claim.

title: Machine-readable architecture findings path
summary: Final architecture review must include a machine-readable findings path or schema proposal.
verification: Confirm S08-FINDINGS.json remains parseable and represented in architecture registry records.
non_claims: No product Legal KnowQL behavior claim.
No managed embedding API fallback claim.

title: Assess FalkorDB text-to-cypher PyO3 route
summary: Future generated-Cypher work must keep R017 active until product Legal KnowQL behavior is separately proven.
verification: Review M003 proof closure and future product Legal KnowQL validation evidence.
non_claims: No product Legal KnowQL behavior claim.
No live legal graph execution claim.

title: Proof artifacts remain redacted and categorical
summary: Proof artifacts must avoid raw provider bodies, credentials, prompts, raw legal text, and raw FalkorDB rows.
verification: Inspect proof artifacts for categorical status and absence of raw provider/legal/runtime payloads.
non_claims: No raw provider body persistence claim.
No credential, prompt, raw legal text, or raw row emission claim.

title: LLM output is not legal authority
summary: Generated Cypher and LLM output must not be treated as legal authority.
verification: Future legal-answering work must cite deterministic source evidence rather than LLM authority.

title: Executable architecture verification workflow
summary: Architecture planning and review must validate architecture registry traceability, anchors, unresolved gates, and forbidden overclaims.
verification: M004 verifier passes on schema, registry, graph, and overclaim checks.
non_claims: Does not itself prove product runtime behavior.

title: Temporal status semantics remain explicit
summary: LegalGraph records must preserve temporal status semantics and avoid mutating prior source revisions when editions or source hashes change.
verification: Future implementation verifies idempotent replay, changed-source revision handling, and new-edition behavior on fixture imports.
non_claims: Does not prove import runtime behavior.
Does not validate same-date conflict policy.

title: Runtime and legal overclaim risk
summary: Architecture documentation can drift into claiming product/runtime/legal behavior not proven by source, runtime, or real-document evidence.
verification: Verifier fails forbidden FalkorDB/vector/full-text/UDF/ODT/parser/retrieval/legal-answer overclaims.
non_claims: Risk item does not assert current product failure.
